text: Autoregressive decoding in Transformer models is frequently constrained by memory bandwidth limitations due to key-value cache loading. Multi-query attention (MQA) has been proposed to alleviate such constraints using a single key-value head. However, its corresponding model performance is generally lower than that of the original Transformer model and requires full from-scratch learning over millions of parameters to obtain small losses, which is computationally expensive. In this work, we show that we can - Up-train an MHA model into an MQA model with very few training epochs (using just 5% of the original pre-training compute), - Generalize multi-head attention (MHA) and multi-query attention (MQA) into a new variant called Grouped-Query Attention (GQA), which supports a wide range of key-value head counts. As such, we find that, by tuning a few hyperparameters, our GQA approach can achieve similar performance to MHA, yet faster inference time than MQA. These results contribute to the development of scalable, low-latency, large-language-models (LLMs).
headline: Fully Human Written
